name: JavaScript Covert-Tactics Scanner
description: Detect covert/evasive tactics in JavaScript/TypeScript: Unicode steganography (invisible/zero-width/bidi chars, confusable-homoglyph punctuation and letters), XOR/char-code/escape string hiding, and environment-keyed conditional behavior (timezone/locale/geo/proxy). Emits STEGO.*/OBF.*/EVADE.* atoms and flags when tactic families co-occur. Static and read-only.

prerequisites.install_hint: Install Python 3.8+ — pure stdlib, no other dependency.

safety.notes: Read-only static scan (regex + Unicode category analysis). Never parses as code, imports, or executes the input.

args.name: input
args.desc: file OR directory of .js/.ts/.mjs/.cjs/.jsx/.tsx to scan

payload.note: Zero-dependency detector (Unicode via stdlib unicodedata + regex). Emits STEGO.*/OBF.*/EVADE.* atoms; the strongest signal is co-occurrence across families (hidden text + obfuscation + environment-keyed branching). Deliberately tactic-focused, not tied to any single incident.
